title: Lifetimes 1
description: Lifetime annotations tell the compiler how the lifetimes of references in function parameters relate to each other.
instructions: Add the correct lifetime annotations to make the function compile.
hints: The compiler can't tell which reference (x or y) will be returned.
Add a lifetime parameter: fn longest<'a>(x: &'a str, y: &'a str) -> &'a str
The lifetime 'a means the returned reference lives at least as long as the shorter input.
explanation: Lifetime annotations describe the relationship between the lifetimes of references. `'a` in `fn longest<'a>(x: &'a str, y: &'a str) -> &'a str` tells the compiler that the returned reference will live at least as long as the shorter of the two input lifetimes. The compiler uses this information to prevent dangling references.
